# criblio_monitor (Resource)

Monitor Resource

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "criblio_monitor" "example" {
  description = "example"
  enabled = "example"
  firing_after = "example"
  id = "example"
  name = "example"
  notification_policies = "example"
  notifications_enabled = "example"
  ok_after = "example"
  params = "example"
  product = "example"
  query = "example"
  rules = "example"
  schedule_interval_seconds = "example"
  silences = "example"
  sql_override = "example"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tools/codegen -->
## Schema

### Required

- `enabled` (Boolean)
- `firing_after` (Number)
- `id` (String)
- `name` (String)
- `ok_after` (Number)
- `params` (String) Monitor parameters as JSON string (use jsonencode({...}) in HCL).
- `product` (String)
- `query` (String) PromQL string or MonitorQuery object as JSON. Use a plain string or jsonencode({...}) in HCL.
- `rules` (String) Array of monitor rules as JSON string (use jsonencode([...]) in HCL).
- `schedule_interval_seconds` (Number)

### Optional

- `description` (String)
- `notification_policies` (List of String)
- `notifications_enabled` (Boolean)
- `silences` (List of String)
- `sql_override` (String) SQL override as JSON string, e.g. jsonencode({instant: '...', range: '...'}).

### Read-Only

- `is_default` (Boolean)

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = criblio_monitor.my_criblio_monitor
  id = "example"
}
```

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import criblio_monitor.my_criblio_monitor "example"
```
